senses.definition: a professional writer who is employed by a publication, media outlet, or organization on a regular basis to produce written content, such as articles, features, or news stories

examples.x: As a staff writer for the magazine, she writes articles on travel and lifestyle.
After working as a freelance journalist, he was hired as a staff writer at the news website.
The staff writer’s piece on environmental issues was well-received by the readers.
He was promoted from a junior reporter to a staff writer at the city’s largest daily paper.
The staff writer worked late into the night to meet the deadline for the special edition.

senses.definition: a journalist who is responsible for revising or reworking existing news stories or information to ensure accuracy, clarity, and proper style before publication

examples.x: The rewrite man worked late into the night, making sure the article was ready for the morning edition.
She was known as the best rewrite man in the newsroom because of her ability to improve any article.
After the reporter filed his story, the rewrite man stepped in to give it a final touch.
The editor assigned the rewrite man to handle the story because it needed a lot of revisions.
Every newsroom relies on a skilled rewrite man to ensure that articles meet editorial standards.

senses.definition: a journalist or editor who specializes in covering political news and events, overseeing the reporting and analysis of political developments, policies, elections, and other related topics

examples.x: After the debate, the political editor analyzed the candidates' performances in an article.
The political editor wrote a detailed report on the latest government policy changes.
In the meeting, the political editor discussed strategies for covering the upcoming referendum.
The newspaper hired a new political editor to cover the upcoming national elections.

senses.definition: someone who writes or talks about the events of the day or a particular subject in a newspaper or on a social platform or TV

examples.x: The news commentator discussed the economic changes, helping viewers understand how it could impact their daily lives.
Many people follow the financial commentator on social media for investment tips and market analysis.
The cultural commentator offered thoughtful critiques on the latest film releases, influencing public opinion.

senses.definition: an individual who provides analysis, opinions, and insights on political matters and current events, often through media platforms such as television, radio, newspapers, or online publications

examples.x: The political commentator explained the implications of the recent election results on the news program.
Many people turn to their favorite political commentator for insights into the current government policies.
As a political commentator, she often appears on talk shows to discuss the latest political developments.
He has built a reputation as a respected political commentator, offering balanced perspectives on controversial issues.
The newspaper hired a political commentator to write regular columns on national and international politics.

senses.definition: a journalist who regularly writes articles on a particular subject for a newspaper or magazine

examples.x: The columnist wrote a compelling opinion piece on environmental conservation.
She is a fashion columnist known for her insights into the latest trends.
The newspaper hired a political columnist to cover the upcoming election.
The columnist's weekly column on technology reviews has a loyal following.
He is a sports columnist who analyzes games and player performances.

senses.definition: someone who prepares news to be broadcast or writes for newspapers, magazines, or news websites

examples.x: A journalist must always verify the facts before writing a story.
As a journalist, he has traveled to many countries.
My sister is a journalist for a major newspaper.
The journalist interviewed the prime minister yesterday.
The journalist spent months researching for his article.

senses.definition: a person who writes stories about the personal lives of celebrities in a newspaper

examples.x: The gossip columnist wrote about the celebrity's latest relationship rumors.
Everyone in the office was reading the latest gossip columnist's column about the actor's unexpected breakup.
Many people enjoy the gossip columnist's articles, even if they know some of the information may not be true.
The gossip columnist made headlines after publishing a story about a scandal involving a popular singer.
He became famous for being the most reliable gossip columnist in the entertainment industry.

senses.definition: a professional who takes and publishes photographs that tell stories and document events, often for news publications
someone who offers expert analysis and insights on news and current events

examples.x: The news analyst explained the economic implications of the government's new policy.
After the debate, the news analyst provided a detailed breakdown of each candidate's performance.
As a news analyst, she often appears on television to provide expert opinions on international relations.
In his column, the news analyst examined how recent events might affect the stock market.

senses.definition: a group of individuals, typically within a publication or media organization, responsible for determining the editorial stance, policy, and content of the publication

examples.x: The editorial board of the newspaper met this morning to discuss their stance on the upcoming election.
Members of the editorial board often debate different viewpoints before settling on the final opinion piece.
The editorial board publishes a weekly column that reflects the publication's views on global issues.
After reviewing the editorial board’s opinion, the readers were left with a clearer understanding of the issue.

senses.definition: a professional who ensures that written material is clear, concise, and free of errors in grammar, spelling, and punctuation
someone employed by a TV or radio station or a newspaper to report news from a particular country or on a particular matter

examples.x: The network's Middle East correspondent provided updates on the conflict in the region.
As the White House correspondent, she covers the latest developments from the nation's capital.
The newspaper assigned a technology correspondent to report on advancements in the tech industry.
The radio station's sports correspondent delivers live commentary from major sporting events.

senses.definition: an entry-level position in a newsroom or publishing setting that involves assisting with various tasks to support the editorial staff

examples.x: The copy boy quickly ran to the newsroom to deliver the editor’s notes.
She started as a copy boy before working her way up to a reporter position.
The copy boy handed the interview transcripts to the editor for review.
At the newspaper, the copy boy’s job was to collect the final stories and bring them to the printing department.

senses.definition: freelance photographers who aggressively pursue and take pictures of celebrities, often in invasive or intrusive ways

examples.x: The paparazzi gathered outside the celebrity's home, hoping to catch a glimpse of the famous actor.
The paparazzi followed the couple everywhere they went, trying to capture candid shots for tabloid magazines.
Despite attempts to avoid attention, the singer was constantly hounded by the paparazzi.
The paparazzi's aggressive pursuit of celebrities has raised concerns about privacy and harassment.
The actress hired security to shield her from the paparazzi while attending the movie premiere.

senses.definition: a journalist who creates content using various media formats, such as text, images, videos, and audio, to tell stories and deliver news across different platforms

examples.x: The multimedia journalist spent the day capturing video footage for the online news story.
The newsroom hired a multimedia journalist to create engaging content for their social media accounts.
As a multimedia journalist, she writes articles, records podcasts, and edits videos for the website.
In today’s digital age, a multimedia journalist needs to know how to use video, audio, and text to tell a story.

senses.definition: someone who is in charge of a newspaper agency, magazine, etc. and decides what should be published

examples.x: The editor reviewed the articles submitted by journalists before publication.
The magazine editor selects the cover story and feature articles for each issue.
She worked her way up from copy editor to become the editor of the lifestyle section.
The editor collaborated with writers and photographers to create compelling content.
He's known for his editorial expertise and sharp eye for detail as an editor.

senses.definition: a newspaper, website, or magazine columnist that replies to e-mails and letters sent by readers and gives them advice

examples.x: People often turn to the advice columnist for suggestions on how to handle relationship issues.
The advice columnist received hundreds of letters every week from readers seeking guidance.
In her latest column, the advice columnist tackled the topic of dealing with difficult coworkers.
The advice columnist's thoughtful response helped the reader feel more confident about making a decision.
After struggling with a tough situation, he decided to write to the advice columnist for some much-needed help.

senses.definition: someone whose job involves reviewing and suppressing obscene or politically unwanted material from a book, movie, etc.
someone who writes a piece to be published in a newspaper or magazine

examples.x: The magazine has a diverse group of contributors who write articles on a wide range of topics.
As a regular contributor to the local newspaper, she enjoys sharing her insights on community events.
The online publication accepts submissions from freelance contributors around the world.
The editor-in-chief praised the contributions of the staff writers and external contributors.
The magazine features a column written by a celebrity contributor each month.

senses.definition: a young and inexperienced newspaper journalist

examples.x: The cub reporter was excited to cover his first story for the local newspaper.
After months of hard work, the cub reporter was promoted to a full-time reporter.
The cub reporter was eager to prove himself by writing a compelling piece for the front page.
Everyone in the newsroom offered advice to the cub reporter as she prepared for her first deadline.

senses.definition: a journalist who writes editorial pieces in the press

examples.x: The editorialist shared their opinion on the new government policy in today’s newspaper.
He became well-known as an editorialist for his bold takes on political matters.
The newspaper’s editorialist argued that the city needs better public transportation.
As an editorialist, her role is to express views that sometimes challenge popular beliefs.

senses.definition: a person whose job is to make a new actor, product, etc. known to the public
a person who gathers and reports news or does interviews for a newspaper, TV, radio station, etc.

examples.x: The reporter interviewed witnesses at the scene of the accident.
She works as a political reporter covering events at the state capitol.
He's an investigative reporter who uncovers corruption and misconduct.
She started her career as a crime reporter, covering stories for the local newspaper.
The reporter attended the press conference to ask questions about the new policy.

senses.definition: a journalist who is not an employee of a newspaper, but who supplies stories for that newspaper from time to time

examples.x: The newspaper relies on a network of stringers to cover local events and breaking news stories.
As a stringer for the magazine, she submits articles on a freelance basis, covering topics of interest to the publication.
Stringers often work irregular hours, attending events and conducting interviews as needed to meet deadlines.
The stringer's role is crucial in providing coverage of events that may not be covered by full-time staff reporters.
Many aspiring journalists start their careers as stringers, gaining valuable experience and building their portfolios.

senses.definition: a professional writer who creates advertising and promotional materials, typically for businesses and organizations
someone who is in charge of the advertising and publicity of a particular actor, musician, etc., providing information to a newspaper, magazine, etc.
an individual or organization responsible for overseeing the production, distribution, and business operations of a magazine

examples.x: He worked as a magazine publisher for over 20 years before starting his own publishing company.
After a successful marketing campaign, the magazine publisher saw a significant rise in subscriptions.
The magazine publisher made sure to feature both local stories and international content in every issue.
The magazine publisher decided to increase the frequency of the issue after noticing growing demand.
As a magazine publisher, she was always looking for new ways to attract readers.

senses.definition: the number of people who read a particular magazine, newspaper, or book on a regular basis

examples.x: The magazine's readership has grown steadily over the past year, thanks to engaging content and targeted marketing efforts.
The newspaper conducted a survey to better understand its readership demographics and preferences.
The author's latest book has attracted a diverse readership, spanning multiple age groups and interests.
The online publication tracks readership metrics to assess the popularity of articles and topics.
The editors strive to cater to their readership's interests by featuring a variety of content in each issue.
